description.content: Manage Databricks clusters for running data engineering and data science workloads on Apache Spark.

Creates a new Spark cluster. This method acquires new instances from the cloud provider and starts the Spark driver and worker processes. The cluster is created asynchronously; use the cluster_id returned to poll for status.

variable.description: The hostname of your Databricks workspace. Format varies by cloud provider (e.g., adb-<workspace-id>.<random>.azuredatabricks.net for Azure, <workspace-id>.cloud.databricks.com for AWS).

response.name: Cluster creation initiated successfully.

Permanently deletes a Spark cluster. If the cluster is running, it is terminated and resources are asynchronously removed. If the cluster is terminated, it is immediately removed. A cluster can only be permanently deleted by an admin or the cluster creator.

Lists runs from most recently started to least. Results are paginated. Runs can be filtered by job, active state, and completion status.

query.description: The job for which to list runs. If omitted, lists runs for all jobs.
If true, only active runs are included in the results; otherwise lists both active and completed runs.
If true, only completed runs are included in the results.
The offset of the first run to return.
The number of runs to return (max 25).
The type of runs to return.
Whether to include task details in each run.
Filter runs started after this time (epoch milliseconds).
Filter runs started before this time (epoch milliseconds).

The hostname of your Databricks workspace. Format varies by cloud provider (e.g., adb-<workspace-id>.<random>.azuredatabricks.net for Azure, <workspace-id>.cloud.databricks.com for AWS).

The Databricks REST API provides programmatic access to manage Databricks workspace resources including clusters, jobs, and workspace objects. All API endpoints require authentication using a personal access token or OAuth token passed via the Authorization header. The base URL is specific to your Databricks workspace deployment region.

Contact Support:
 Name: Databricks
 Email: support@databricks.com
